70 MPH WIND GUSTS AND PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL CAUSED A VARIETY OF DAMAGE. WINDOW SCREENS WERE SHREDDED, SOME WINDOWS WERE BROKEN, SIDING WAS DAMAGED ON HOUSE AND CAMPER. IN ADDITION, NEARBY POTATO FIELD WAS SHREDDED, AND CORN WAS ABOUT 50 PERCENT DAMAGED. 1.70 INCHES OF RAIN ALSO FELL.

COTTONWOOD TREES BLOWN OVER. SAME INDIVIDUAL REPORTED THE GROUND WAS WHITE WITH HAIL 4 MILES EAST OF KARLSRUHE, AND ALL HIS HAY THERE WAS BLOWN WAY. REPORT WAS RELAYED TO THE NWS BY A BROADCAST MEDIA PARTNER.
RELAYED BY BROADCAST MEDIA PARTNER. MOST HAIL WAS SMALLER, BUT WERE A FEW GOLF BALLS.

REPORT FROM AUGUST 30TH STORM. VARIOUS RESIDENTS OF THE AREA WERE GREATLY DAMAGED FROM BASEBALL SIZE HAIL. A COUPLE OF CAMPERS WERE DAMAGED, WINDOWS ON HOMES WERE BROKEN, AND SIDING WAS DESTROYED.
REPORT FROM AUGUST 30 STORM. MULTIPLE VEHICLES WERE HAIL DAMAGED. CANOLA CROP NEARBY WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED.

REPORT FROM AUGUST 30 STORM. HAIL WAS BETWEEN HEN EGG AND TENNIS BALL IN SIZE. YARD WAS WHITE WITH HAIL, AS IT HAILED FOR ABOUT ONE-HALF HOUR. 2.75 INCHES OF RAIN ALSO FELL. SOYBEAN CROP WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED.

SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF OVERTURNED SEMI ON HIGHWAY 21 EAST OF FLASHER AT 5:56 PM CDT. LOCATION ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA AT TIME OF REPORT.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF SEMI BLOWN OVER ON HIGHWAY 21 EAST OF FLASHER AT 556 PM CDT. INCIDENT VERIFIED BY DISPATCH. LOCATION ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA AT TIME OF REPORT.
REPORT FROM AUGUST 30 STORM. WIND GUST WAS MEASURED WHILE HELPING AN INDIVIDUAL IN AN OVERTURNED SEMI-TRUCK. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE EVEN HIGHER WHEN THE SEMI-TRUCK OVERTURNED.
PHOTO RELAYED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA, INCLUDING SMASHED WINDSHIELD OF CAR. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR BUT REPORT INCLUDED VERY LARGE HAIL ALONG HIGHWAY 8 BETWEEN HETTINGER AND MOTT.
REPORT FROM AUGUST 30 STORM. CROPS WERE DAMAGED AND SOME WINDOWS WERE BROKEN. ADDITIONAL NEIGHBORS ALSO HAD BROKEN WINDOWS.

REPORT FROM AUGUST 30 STORMS. TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL COMBINED WITH 60 MPH WIND GUSTS TO CAUSE EXTENSIVE DAMAGE. ALL WINDOWS WERE BROKEN OUT ON NORTH SIDE OF TWO HOMES. DENTED STEEL SIDING ON ONE, AND DESTROYED VINYL SIDING ON THE OTHER. FIVE VEHICLE WINDOWS WERE BROKEN. CROPS WERE FLATTENED AND TREES WERE STRIPPED OF LEAVES. THE WIND GRADUALLY SWITCHED FROM NORTHWEST TO NORTHEAST THROUGH THE STORM.
REPORT FROM AUGUST 30TH STORM. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. HAILED FOR ABOUT ONE HALF HOUR. CROP WAS DESTROYED.

REPORT FROM AUGUST 30TH STORM. NOT MUCH WIND, BUT LARGE HAIL PUNCHED MANY HOLES THROUGH THE METAL ROOFS ON POLE BARNS. FIVE VEHICLES WERE SUBSTANTIALLY DAMAGED. HAIL HIT HARD ENOUGH TO CLEAN SPOTS OFF ON CONCRETE DRIVEWAY APRON. BIRDBATH WAS BROKEN UP, AND HOLES WERE PUNCHED IN VINYL HOT TUB COVER. SOME WINDOWS WERE BROKEN ON POLE BARNS, AND EXHAUST VENT FOR POLE BARN FURNACE WAS BUSTED OFF.
REPORT FROM AUGUST 30TH STORM. WIND DRIVEN HAIL NEAR THE HIDDENWOOD HUNT TEEPEE SITE SHREDDED A VERY NICE LOOKING FIELD OF EIGHT FOOT HIGH CORN DOWN TO KNEE HIGH.
HAIL RANGING IN SIZE FROM MARBLE TO BASEBALL FELL FROM 600 TO 615 PM MDT. REPORT RELAYED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

WIND GUSTS OF 60 MPH WITH ENOUGH PEA SIZE HAIL THAT DRIFTS OF HAIL REMAINED AND LEAVES WERE STRIPPED FROM THE TREES IN THE YARD. THE STRONG WINDS AND HAIL LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES UNTIL APPROXIMATELY 751 AM CDT.
MUCH WIND-DRIVEN HAIL FELL. MOST WAS SMALLER BUT SOME WAS THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. A SKYLIGHT ON A HOME WAS BROKEN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MOSTLY PEA SIZED HAIL BUT A FEW QUARTERS.
